title: rootless: fix hang on startup with older glibc versions
body:
The issue is caused by the Go Runtime that messes up with the process signals, overriding SIGSETXID and SIGCANCEL which are used internally by glibc.  They are used to inform all the threads to update their stored uid/gid information.  This causes a hang on the set*id glibc wrappers since the handler installed by glibc is never invoked.

Since we are running with only one thread, we don't really need to update other threads or even the current thread as we are not using getuid/getgid before the execvp.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1625
